tasks.description: Initialize execution and acquire the analyst-provided ticker universe for the Consumer Staples Fund. Establish working context, constraints, and success criteria so the portfolio build adheres to net +30%, gross ≤200%, 15–20 longs, and 10–15 shorts while prioritizing quality over quantity and CIO-level rigor.

subtasks.description: Initialize structured planning context and restate the fund’s mandate, hard constraints, and evaluation criteria (alpha potential, low beta, diversification, factor-aware). Ensure all subsequent actions are benchmarked against these constraints.

subtasks.description: Validate the analyst picks: filter for conviction ≥ 0.5, de-duplicate tickers, confirm each security is Consumer Staples, and flag any missing fields (e.g., absent valuation snapshot). Create a clean working list of candidate longs and shorts.
Map candidates to the hard count constraints: target a working set of 15–20 longs and 10–15 shorts for Portfolio V1. Note where counts are under/over the bounds to address later in iteration.

tasks.description: Construct Portfolio V1 exclusively from the analyst picks that pass the conviction and sector checks. Encode positions and preliminary allocations consistent with constraints while deferring any portfolio analytics until after V1 is set (per hard constraint).

subtasks.description: Select 15–20 high-conviction longs and 10–15 shorts from the validated list, prioritizing quality, durability of FCF, balance sheet strength, and clarity of near-term catalysts. Keep a reserve list for potential substitutions.

Define preliminary risk-budgeted allocations such that the intended target is net ≈ +30% and gross ≤ 200% after normalization. As a starting point, aim for longs ≈ 115% and shorts ≈ 85% gross to achieve net ≈ +30% (to be refined later).

Create a Portfolio V1 portfolio_dict mapping each ticker to {allocation, position}, where allocation is the risk allocation (decimal) and position is long/short. Keep allocations within reasonable per-name caps to facilitate later optimization.
Document the rationale for each inclusion (tying back to analyst thesis and CIO judgment) and record any open questions to resolve during diligence (data gaps, thesis conflicts, timing).
Checkpoint: verify Portfolio V1 exists before any portfolio analysis tools are used. Confirm the portfolio meets count constraints; exact net/gross will be tuned later.

tasks.description: Run exhaustive, evidence-based diligence on every V1 ticker (and reserve candidates) to validate fundamentals and newsflow. Gather primary financials, ratios, estimates, and up-to-date company news/transcripts to support CIO judgment without hallucinating any data.

subtasks.description: For each ticker, fetch fundamentals: income statement, balance sheet, cash flow, financial ratios, and analyst estimates. Extract durable FCF generation, margin resilience/expansion, leverage, and capital allocation signals. Note any missing datasets explicitly.
